patch.value: pm validate after fresh create is scary — downgrade default profile noise

Dogfood 2026-05-17: a brand-new agent creating a minimal item (just title + description) then running `pm validate` sees ok:false with missing_acceptance_criteria, missing_estimated_minutes, missing_reviewer warnings. This is intimidating and pushes agents into anti-patterns.

Proposal: default metadata_profile in settings should treat these as `info` not `warn`, OR pm init should pick a relaxed profile and document opt-in to stricter profiles via `pm config validate.profile strict`.

Acceptance: pm validate on a fresh tracker with bare items returns ok:true (or ok:true with `info` rows). Tests cover default profile + opting into strict.

Implemented pm-tylj partial: pm validate metadata-check now zero-suppresses count fields and omits per-field item_ids/truncated when no items missing. Lifecycle/resolution checks still emit zero-count placeholders (follow-up). Removes ~10-30 lines per fresh-init run.
